assumptions: The intragroup dividend receivable is a foreign-currency-denominated monetary item in the consolidated financial statements.
The question is about possible IFRS hedge accounting models for the foreign exchange exposure reflected in consolidated accounts.

approaches.label: Foreign exchange in profit or loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraphs 5.7.2 and B5.7.2 state that foreign exchange gains and losses on monetary financial assets and liabilities are recognised in profit or loss unless a hedge accounting exception applies.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph B5.7.2 expressly identifies a cash flow hedge as a hedge accounting route that changes the normal profit or loss treatment of foreign exchange differences on monetary items.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 5.7.3 states that gains and losses on hedged items in a hedging relationship are recognised in accordance with the hedge accounting requirements.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 specifically addresses hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements for foreign currency risk on a net investment in a foreign operation, with effective hedge results recognised in other comprehensive income and reclassified on disposal.
